authoritative_sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual, Sector 56 and Subsector 561 Administrative and Support Services
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Census Bureau and Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The official NAICS manual defines the industry as routine day-to-day support activities for other organizations and distinguishes support-service work from strategic management, public administration, complete client operation, and single-service specialist industries.

authoritative_sources.title: Administrative and Support Services: NAICS 561
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BLS is the official federal labor-statistics source for NAICS 561 operating context, workforce terminology, and industry injury and illness indicators.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 9001:2015 Quality management systems - Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO 9001 is the recognized international quality-management system standard and supports review, documented information, monitoring, nonconformity, corrective action, and continual-improvement workflow claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality review, nonconformance, and corrective-action handling

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 10002:2018 Quality management - Customer satisfaction - Guidelines for complaints handling in organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO 10002 is recognized complaints-handling guidance for products and services, directly relevant to client complaints and service-quality nonconformance in business services.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: complaint intake, review, analysis, and improvement handling

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 20000-1:2018 Information technology - Service management - Service management system requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: International Organization for Standardization and International Electrotechnical Commission
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO/IEC 20000-1 is a recognized service-management system standard for service planning, design, transition, delivery, monitoring, review, and improvement, which fits service-quality evidence handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: service-quality lifecycle and SLA evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 19011:2018 Guidelines for auditing management systems
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO 19011 is recognized management-system audit guidance and supports audit evidence handling, audit-program discipline, and the boundary between quality-review support and final audit opinion.

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government, GAO-25-107721
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book is an authoritative public internal-control framework for control activities, information and communication, monitoring, deficiencies, and remediation evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control verification, deficiency, and remediation review

authoritative_sources.title: Universal Electronic Records Management Requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. National Archives and Records Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the federal records authority, and its electronic records requirements support capture, metadata, retention, access, disposition, and auditability expectations for service-quality evidence repositories.

authoritative_sources.title: SOC 2 - SOC for Service Organizations: Trust Services Criteria
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA SOC 2 is a recognized service-organization assurance reference for security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy controls, which are common client audit dimensions in outsourced business services.

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations, SP 800-53 Rev. 5
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 is an authoritative public control catalog for access, audit, privacy, incident response, and system integrity evidence in information systems that hold business-service records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: privacy, access, audit, and incident-control review

authoritative_sources.title: OSHA Injury and Illness Recordkeeping and Reporting Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the U.S. workplace safety authority. Its recordkeeping guidance supports escalation when a quality incident includes workplace injury or illness evidence requiring safety-owner handling.

workflow_stages.name: scope and prerequisite classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that the request concerns administrative support or business-services quality work, then validate tenant service model, client SOW, service catalog, SOP, quality plan, acceptance criteria, approval matrix, and systems-of-record context.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: quality evidence collection and traceability review
workflow_stages.description: Index service tickets, work orders, sampled cases, monitored contacts, queue logs, reviewer notes, defect classifications, complaint records, SLA evidence, audit logs, and record-location metadata before quality conclusions are made.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: inspection, monitoring, and findings assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare sampled service work to acceptance criteria, quality plan, SOP, SLA, and client SOW requirements; identify pass, defect, nonconformance, unresolved risk, or evidence gap posture.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: hold, release, and nonconformance boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve hold and release controls, block unsupported closure, route final signoff to authorized owners, and document evidence-backed release, hold, or nonconformance recommendations.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: defect, complaint, incident, and corrective-action escalation
workflow_stages.description: Summarize repeat defects, complaints, incidents, nonconformances, CAPA plans, and effectiveness-check gaps while routing safety, security, HR, legal, finance, or formal QA authority to adjacent owners.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: client audit and service-organization evidence handling
workflow_stages.description: Package control evidence, processing-integrity gaps, privacy/access issues, audit trails, open assumptions, and unresolved conflicts for the control owner or audit lead without claiming SOC, ISO, or final audit signoff.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: client SOW, service catalog, quality plan, SOP, work instruction, SLA, KPI definition, acceptance criteria, approval matrix, and escalation matrix
service tickets, work orders, case records, queue logs, document packets, monitored contacts, call-quality forms, correspondence, and client acknowledgments
sampling plans, sampled items, review notes, defect classifications, error taxonomies, quality scorecards, SLA dashboards, complaint records, and audit findings
hold tags, release requests, rework records, second-review signoffs, nonconformance records, CAPA records, root-cause notes, effectiveness checks, and unresolved-risk logs
record-location metadata, document-control versions, retention classes, legal-hold flags, redaction logs, access-review evidence, audit logs, and service-organization control evidence
systems_of_record: incident management and safety reporting system

allowed: classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
evaluate whether visible quality evidence supports release recommendation, hold, review, investigation, nonconformance, corrective action, or escalation
prepare quality status summaries, findings summaries, hold or release records, nonconformance records, CAPA escalations, incident escalations, and unresolved-risk notes
map tenant-specific service artifacts and retrieval hooks while preserving public support-service and quality-management boundaries
escalate: legal interpretation, client-contract approval, regulator negotiation, formal audit opinion, SOC or ISO certification, and executive policy override
safety-critical signoff, OSHA recordability determination, data-security incident response, privacy breach determination, HR placement decision, finance approval, or licensed professional judgment
tenant policy, public-source, client SOW, SOP, approval-matrix, or system-of-record conflict that materially changes release, hold, or closure posture
missing service model, acceptance criteria, quality plan, source records, sampled items, queue owner, reviewer support, or evidence owner that would change the quality conclusion
refuse: clear a hold, close a finding, release a service batch, or mark CAPA effective without required evidence and authority
bypass documented hold, release, safety, privacy, access, retention, SLA, SOW, or quality-control boundaries
treat administrative support and business services as a cosmetic label for generic quality advice or complete client-operations ownership
claim final audit, legal, safety, HR, finance, security, or certification authority from this lane

domain_failure_modes: service work is released while hold, complaint, nonconformance, or second-review evidence remains unresolved
quality findings are closed without sampled workpaper, reviewer notes, CAPA evidence, or effectiveness checks
repeat defects are treated as isolated errors and not escalated for corrective action
client SOW, SOP, service catalog, and SLA evidence conflict and the lane invents a confident release answer
ticket, queue, QMS, CRM, IAM, and audit records disagree and the lane fails to preserve traceability
a workplace injury, privacy/access issue, HR placement decision, finance approval, legal interpretation, or formal audit question is handled as routine quality work

the pack stays generic and fails to reference business-services artifacts such as SOWs, service catalogs, service tickets, work queues, sampling plans, complaint records, and service-organization controls

required_workflow_question_answers.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: Client SOWs, service catalogs, SOPs, quality plans, acceptance criteria, work orders, tickets, queue records, sampled cases, quality scorecards, complaint records, hold or release records, nonconformance records, CAPA trackers, SLA dashboards, QMS, CRM, document repositories, IAM extracts, GRC/control repositories, and audit logs govern the lane.

required_workflow_question_answers.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: The key differences are outsourced or shared support-service boundaries, client SOW and SLA evidence, high-volume queue and sampled-work artifacts, cross-client confidentiality, service-organization control evidence, and adjacent single-service specialists such as HR, finance, legal, security, facilities, records, and formal audit.

required_workflow_question_answers.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: The lane cannot make legal, contract, safety-critical, HR placement, payroll, finance, security-incident, privacy-breach, formal audit, certification-body, licensed professional, executive policy, or final client approval decisions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority
Evidence is incomplete for release or signoff
A user tries to close quality work without review support

notes: The public baseline constrains terminology, workflow, and evidence posture, but tenant retrieval is still required for each client SOW, quality plan, SOP, service catalog, acceptance criteria, hold or release matrix, approval owner, and system-of-record map.

No blocking ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because unresolved jurisdictional, safety, privacy, HR, finance, legal, contract, and certification questions are explicit orchestrator-return conditions.
